## Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet

Date: Monday, June 1, 2026

Page 1 of 1

Attachments: No

Item:

Introduction of Animal Services Director

Initiated By:

Tia Foula, Assistant County Manager

Action Proposed:

Information Only
chunks.heading: Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet

chunks.text: ## Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet
Introduction of Animal Services Director

## Description:

It is my pleasure to introduce Ashley Howell as Nash County's new Animal Services Director.

Ms. Howell brings more than five years of progressive experience in animal welfare, animal control, and shelter leadership. She began her career in North Carolina, working in animal control before advancing into shelter management leadership roles in Virginia.

Most recently, she served as Animal Shelter Manager for the Page County Animal Shelter, where she oversaw shelter operations, staff supervision, animal care services, public outreach, and community programming. Throughout her career, she has demonstrated a strong commitment to improving outcomes for shelter animals while building strong relationships with rescue organizations, community partners, and the public.

Ms. Howell has worked extensively with rescue and transfer partners to expand lifesaving opportunities for animals in need and has been a strong advocate for spay and neuter initiatives and community-based programs aimed at reducing pet overpopulation and increasing access to affordable services for pet owners.

We are excited to welcome Ms. Howell to Nash County and look forward to the leadership, compassion, and energy she will bring to Animal Services as we continue strengthening services, partnerships, and care for the animals and residents of Nash County.

Please join me in welcoming Ashley Howell to Nash County.

Tia Foula, Assistant County Manager

Action Proposed:

Information Only
chunks.heading: Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet

chunks.text: ## Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet
Introduction to the Nash County Public Health Director

## Description:

Good morning. It is my pleasure to introduce our new Public Health Director, Angela Manning.

Angela Manning brings more than 22 years of experience in local public health, including extensive leadership experience serving communities in North Carolina. Most recently, she served as Deputy Health Director, where she helped oversee public health operations, programs, staff leadership, and community health initiatives.

Throughout her career, she has demonstrated a strong commitment to public service, community health improvement, staff development, and collaborative leadership. Her background includes experience in environmental health, public health administration, program oversight, and strategic leadership, all of which will be valuable as we continue strengthening public health services in Nash County.

She joins us at an important time for our community, and I am confident that her experience, professionalism, and passion for public health will complement the strong team already in place within our Health Department.

We are excited to welcome her to Nash County and look forward to the leadership, partnership, and vision she will bring as we continue working to improve the health and well-being of our residents.

Please join me in welcoming Angela Manning to Nash County.
chunks.heading: Description:

chunks.text: Good morning. It is my pleasure to introduce our new Public Health Director, Angela Manning.

Angela Manning brings more than 22 years of experience in local public health, including extensive leadership experience serving communities in North Carolina. Throughout her career, she has demonstrated a strong commitment to public service, community health improvement, staff development, and collaborative leadership. Her background includes experience in environmental health, public health administration, program oversight, and strategic leadership, all of which will be valuable as we continue strengthening public health services in Nash County.

She joins us at an important time for our community, and I am confident that her experience, professionalism, and passion for public health will complement the strong team already in place within our Health Department.
We are excited to welcome her to Nash County and look forward to the leadership, partnership, and vision she will bring as we continue working to improve the health and well-being of our residents.
Please join me in welcoming Angela Manning to Nash County.

## Recommended Action:

At this time, Ms. Manning will take her Oath of Office as Nash County Public Health Director.

PETA Animal Welfare Advocacy Presentation

Initiated By:

Jonathan Boone, Deputy County Manager

Action Proposed:

Information Only
chunks.heading: Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet

chunks.text: ## Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet
PETA Animal Welfare Advocacy Presentation

## Description:

Rachel Bellis, Director of Local Affairs with PETA contacted Commissioner Howell in late April 2026, requesting an opportunity to address the Board of Commissioners regarding animal welfare in Nash County.  In addition to discussing her desire to see Nash County enact a chaining ban, she would also like to address the Board of Commissioners regarding a mandatory spay/neuter program prior to the adoption of animals at the Pet Connection Center.  Staff completed a virtual call with Ms. Bellis on May 12, 2026, to discuss these items, to provide a general overview of current county policy, and to discuss initiatives proposed in Animal Services for FY2027.
chunks.heading: Description:

chunks.text: Rachel Bellis, Director of Local Affairs with PETA contacted Commissioner Howell in late April 2026, requesting an opportunity to address the Board of Commissioners regarding animal welfare in Nash County.  In addition to discussing her desire to see Nash County enact a chaining ban, she would also like to address the Board of Commissioners regarding a mandatory spay/neuter program prior to the adoption of animals at the Pet Connection Center.  Staff completed a virtual call with Ms. Bellis on May 12, 2026, to discuss these items, to provide a general overview of current county policy, and to discuss initiatives proposed in Animal Services for FY2027.

## Recommended Action:

Receive information for further consideration and follow up.
